label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking how the students performed in the experiment described in the passage. I need to look through the text to find the relevant information. The passage mentions an experiment where 143 undergraduate students were asked to write down advice to themselves before doing puzzles. Half used first-person, the other half used second-person. The results stated that those who used second-person completed more puzzles and had a more positive attitude. Then, when it came to writing advice about exercising, again, the second-person group was more positive and planned to exercise more. Looking at the options: A) First-person had better attitude. But the passage says the opposite, so A is incorrect. B) Talking to themselves completed most puzzles. But the study was about writing, not talking. Also, the correct group was second-person writers, not talking. So B is incorrect. C) Encouraged themselves by writing in second-person were better. This matches the passage's findings. They did more puzzles and were more positive. So C is likely correct.
